title: • Today's Intention: I will try to see the world through the eyes of so
content:
• Today's Intention: I will try to see the world through the eyes of someone with whom I disagree, cultivating empathy.
    • NIGHT
    • Night Reading: "He saw them all, all these people, and he felt a kinship with them. He did not envy them, he did not despise them. He loved them."
    • Reflection: How did my own pain today connect me to the pain of others?
    Day 26
    MORNING
    • Morning Reading: "He bent over the water, and saw his own face reflected in it, and in this reflected face there was something that reminded him of another face... the face of his father, the Brahmin."
    • Today's Intention: I will look for the unity in a seemingly chaotic or difficult situation today.
    NIGHT
    • Night Reading: "And when Siddhartha listened attentively to this river, to this song of a thousand voices... but heard them all, the whole, the unity; then the great song of a thousand voices consisted of one single word: Om—perfection."
    • Reflection: Where did I find harmony and unity today? What small moment felt "perfect"?
context: From document: A 30-Day Journey with Siddhartha: Aligning the Path with Daily Life — chunk 25 (chunk 25/29)
